{{Short description="SI unit of electric charge; magnitude of charge of a proton}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Elementary charge
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Electric charge]], [[Electron]], [[Proton]], [[International System of Units]]
| symbol = e
| value = 1.602176634×10⁻¹⁹ C (exact by definition)
| unit = [[Coulomb]]
}}

The '''elementary charge''', usually denoted <math>e</math>, is the [[electric charge]] carried by a single [[proton]] or, equivalently, the magnitude of the negative electric charge carried by a single [[electron]]. It is a fundamental [[physical constant]] and the quantum of electric charge.

:<math>e = 1.602176634 \times 10^{-19}\ \text{C}</math>

Since 2019, the elementary charge has been fixed at this exact value in the [[International System of Units]], defining the [[ampere]] and [[coulomb]].

== Charge quantization ==
All observed electric charges are integer multiples of <math>e</math>. [[Quark]]s have fractional charges (<math>\pm 1/3\,e</math> and <math>\pm 2/3\,e</math>), but due to [[colour confinement]], they are never observed in isolation — all free particles have integer multiples of <math>e</math>.

== Role in fundamental constants ==

=== Fine-structure constant ===
The dimensionless [[fine-structure constant]] <math>\alpha</math> is built from <math>e</math>:
:<math>\alpha = \frac{e^2}{4\pi\varepsilon_0 \hbar c} \approx \frac{1}{137}</math>

=== Coulomb's law ===
:<math>F = k_e\frac{q_1 q_2}{r^2}</math> where <math>k_e = \frac{1}{4\pi\varepsilon_0}</math>

=== Bohr model ===
The [[Bohr radius]] and [[Rydberg constant]] depend on <math>e</math>.

== Experimental determination ==
The elementary charge was first measured by [[Robert Millikan]] and [[Harvey Fletcher]] in the [[oil drop experiment]] (1909), measuring the charge on individual oil droplets. They found that charges were always integer multiples of a fundamental value, establishing the quantization of charge.

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Coulomb]]
* [[Electric charge]]
* [[Electron]]
* [[Proton]]
* [[Quark]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Physical constants]]
[[Category:Electromagnetism]]
[[Category:Electric charge]]
